metadata.description: Execution-based eval pairs for AskChokro benchmarking — v2.0 (100+ pairs)

pairs.question: Show me all users
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users

pairs.question: Show me all users limit 3
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 3

pairs.question: Who is Alice?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = 'Alice'

pairs.question: List all products under $15
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price < 15

pairs.question: Find pending orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'

pairs.question: Show completed orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

pairs.question: List all hardware products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'hardware'

pairs.question: Find products with more than 100 stock
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE stock_quantity > 100

pairs.question: Show refunded orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'refunded'

pairs.question: Find reviews with rating 5
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE rating = 5

pairs.question: Show me all electronics products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'electronics'

pairs.question: List all businesses
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM businesses

pairs.question: Total revenue from completed orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

pairs.question: How many products do we have?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products

pairs.question: Average order value
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders

pairs.question: Total stock across all products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT SUM(stock_quantity) FROM products

pairs.question: How many orders are pending?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'

pairs.question: Average product price
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT AVG(price) FROM products

pairs.question: Most expensive product price
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT MAX(price) FROM products

pairs.question: Cheapest product price
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT MIN(price) FROM products

pairs.question: How many users are there?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users

pairs.question: Average review rating
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT AVG(rating) FROM reviews

pairs.question: Total revenue from all orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders

pairs.question: Count of reviews
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reviews

pairs.question: Revenue per product category
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.category, SUM(order_items.quantity * order_items.price) FROM products JOIN order_items ON products.id = order_items.product_id GROUP BY products.category

pairs.question: Number of orders per status
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY status

pairs.question: What did Alice order?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT products.name FROM products JOIN order_items ON products.id = order_items.product_id JOIN orders ON order_items.order_id = orders.id JOIN users ON orders.user_id = users.id WHERE users.name = 'Alice'

pairs.question: List items in order 1
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name, order_items.quantity FROM order_items JOIN products ON order_items.product_id = products.id WHERE order_items.order_id = 1

pairs.question: Who bought a Widget?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT users.name FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id JOIN order_items ON orders.id = order_items.order_id JOIN products ON order_items.product_id = products.id WHERE products.name = 'Widget'

pairs.question: Total revenue per user
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, SUM(orders.total_amount) FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id GROUP BY users.name

pairs.question: Show product names and their average ratings
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name, AVG(reviews.rating) FROM products JOIN reviews ON products.id = reviews.product_id GROUP BY products.name

pairs.question: Show users and their total number of orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, COUNT(orders.id) FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id GROUP BY users.name

pairs.question: Which products have been reviewed?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT products.name FROM products JOIN reviews ON products.id = reviews.product_id

pairs.question: Show reviews with product names and user names
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, products.name, reviews.rating FROM reviews JOIN users ON reviews.user_id = users.id JOIN products ON reviews.product_id = products.id

pairs.question: List all orders with user name and status
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, orders.status, orders.total_amount FROM orders JOIN users ON orders.user_id = users.id

pairs.question: Show products ordered by Alice
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT products.name FROM products JOIN order_items ON products.id = order_items.product_id JOIN orders ON order_items.order_id = orders.id JOIN users ON orders.user_id = users.id WHERE users.name = 'Alice'

pairs.question: Revenue per product
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name, SUM(order_items.quantity * order_items.price) FROM products JOIN order_items ON products.id = order_items.product_id GROUP BY products.name

pairs.question: Which users have not placed any orders?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name FROM users LEFT JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id WHERE orders.id IS NULL

pairs.question: Show businesses and their total orders count
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT businesses.name, COUNT(orders.id) FROM businesses JOIN orders ON businesses.id = orders.business_id GROUP BY businesses.name

pairs.question: Average order value per user
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, AVG(orders.total_amount) FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id GROUP BY users.name

pairs.question: Orders from today
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE date(created_at) = date('now')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE DATE(created_at) = CURRENT_DATE

pairs.question: Users who joined this month
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', 'now')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE date_trunc('month', created_at) = date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)

pairs.question: Orders placed in the last 7 days
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', '-7 days')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

pairs.question: Revenue this year
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y', created_at) = strftime('%Y', 'now')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE date_trunc('year', created_at) = date_trunc('year', CURRENT_DATE)

pairs.question: Orders in the last 30 days
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', '-30 days')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'

pairs.question: Users who joined in the last 7 days
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', '-7 days')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

pairs.question: Revenue last month
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', datetime('now', '-1 month'))
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE date_trunc('month', created_at) = date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month')

pairs.question: Reviews submitted this week
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', '-7 days')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

pairs.question: Orders from the last 90 days
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= datetime('now', '-90 days')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'

pairs.question: Orders placed this month
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', 'now')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE date_trunc('month', created_at) = date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)

pairs.question: How many orders were placed today?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE date(created_at) = date('now')
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE DATE(created_at) = CURRENT_DATE

pairs.question: Show orders grouped by month
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) AS month, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', created_at)
pairs.postgresExpectedSql: SELECT date_trunc('month', created_at) AS month, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY date_trunc('month', created_at)

pairs.question: Show all my products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE business_id = 1

pairs.question: Who are my users?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE business_id = 1

pairs.question: Total revenue for my business
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE business_id = 1 AND status = 'completed'

pairs.question: My pending orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending' AND business_id = 1

pairs.question: My hardware products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE business_id = 1 AND category = 'hardware'

pairs.question: How many orders do I have?
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE business_id = 1

pairs.question: My average order value
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE business_id = 1

pairs.question: My reviews
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE business_id = 1

pairs.question: My completed orders this month
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE business_id = 1 AND status = 'completed' AND strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) = strftime('%Y-%m', 'now')

pairs.question: Total products in my store
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE business_id = 1

pairs.question: Show the 5 most expensive products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 5

pairs.question: Show the cheapest products first
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC

pairs.question: Latest 5 orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5

pairs.question: Oldest user
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1

pairs.question: Top 3 highest value orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY total_amount DESC LIMIT 3

pairs.question: Products sorted by name
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY name ASC

pairs.question: Most recent review
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM reviews ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1

pairs.question: Best rated products
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name, AVG(reviews.rating) AS avg_rating FROM products JOIN reviews ON products.id = reviews.product_id GROUP BY products.name ORDER BY avg_rating DESC

pairs.question: Users sorted by most orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, COUNT(orders.id) AS order_count FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id GROUP BY users.name ORDER BY order_count DESC

pairs.question: Top spending user
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name, SUM(orders.total_amount) AS total_spent FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id GROUP BY users.name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1

pairs.question: Products that have never been ordered
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM order_items)

pairs.question: Users who have spent more than the average order value
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name FROM users JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id WHERE orders.total_amount > (SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders)

pairs.question: Products with a rating above average
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name FROM products WHERE id IN (SELECT product_id FROM reviews GROUP BY product_id HAVING AVG(rating) > (SELECT AVG(rating) FROM reviews))

pairs.question: Orders worth more than the average
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE total_amount > (SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders)

pairs.question: Products that have been reviewed
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM reviews)

pairs.question: Users who have placed at least 2 orders
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT users.name FROM users WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE orders.user_id = users.id) >= 2

pairs.question: Most popular product by order count
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name FROM products WHERE id = (SELECT product_id FROM order_items GROUP BY product_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1)

pairs.question: Show the highest rated product
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT products.name FROM products WHERE id = (SELECT product_id FROM reviews GROUP BY product_id ORDER BY AVG(rating) DESC LIMIT 1)

pairs.question: Businesses with more than 2 users
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT businesses.name FROM businesses WHERE id IN (SELECT business_id FROM users GROUP BY business_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 2)

pairs.question: Products that cost more than any hardware product
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT MAX(price) FROM products WHERE category = 'hardware')

pairs.question: Show me orders with amount between 10 and 50
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE total_amount BETWEEN 10 AND 50

pairs.question: Find users whose name starts with A
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE 'A%'

pairs.question: Products with no reviews
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM reviews)

pairs.question: Show distinct order statuses
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT status FROM orders

pairs.question: Products in stock (quantity greater than 0)
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE stock_quantity > 0

pairs.question: Orders where total is exactly 10
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE total_amount = 10

pairs.question: Users with gmail email addresses
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@gmail.com%'

pairs.question: Count of products per category
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM products GROUP BY category

pairs.question: Orders that are not completed
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status != 'completed'

pairs.question: Show reviews with a comment
pairs.expectedSql: SELECT * FROM reviews WHERE comment IS NOT NULL
